test_name: 065. Life on the International Space Station

content.cn_text: 你将听到一位工科学生正在就一项研究项目进行演讲，该项目内容是
content.en_text: You will hear an engineering student giving a presentation about a research project on

content.cn_text: 国际空间站的设计特点。
content.en_text: design features of the International Space Station.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间来查看问题31到40。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听并回答问题31到40。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 在我的演讲中，我将总结一项关于
content.en_text: For my presentation, I'm going to summarize the findings of a research project on

content.cn_text: 国际空间站在运行初期的研究项目的发现。
content.en_text: the International Space Station in the early days of its operation.

content.cn_text: 可以说，这是它早期遇到的问题。
content.en_text: It's teething problems as it were.

content.cn_text: 我将首先介绍一些背景信息。
content.en_text: I'll start by giving a little background information.

content.cn_text: 空间站的宇航员本质上是一个不断变化的科学家团队，
content.en_text: The Space Station crew were essentially a constantly changing team of scientists

content.cn_text: 他们来自不同的国家，每次在空间站上居住几个月。
content.en_text: from different countries who lived on the Space Station for several months at a time.

content.cn_text: 他们进行了许多实验，包括一些对太空生活条件的研究，
content.en_text: And they conducted a number of experiments including some studies of living conditions in space

content.cn_text: 这也是我接下来要关注的。
content.en_text: which is what I'm going to be looking at now.

content.cn_text: 那么，在空间站非常有限的条件下，船上的生活是怎样的呢？
content.en_text: So, what was life like on board in the very confined conditions of the Space Station?

content.cn_text: 嗯，宇航员记录的早期印象与人们预期的非常不同。
content.en_text: Well, the early impressions recorded by the astronauts were very different from what people had expected.

content.cn_text: 他们曾预期空气会闻起来不新鲜或人工合成。
content.en_text: They'd expected that the air would smell stale or artificial.

content.cn_text: 但宇航员发现它是新鲜的，这是一个非常积极的发现。
content.en_text: But the astronauts found that it was fresh, so that was one very positive finding.

content.cn_text: 尽管他们身处如此狭小的空间，人们担心会非常热，
content.en_text: And although they were in such a confined space and people worried that it would get very hot,

content.cn_text: 事实上，他们发现调节温度非常容易，可以保持在舒适的范围内。
content.en_text: in fact, they found that it was very easy to turn the temperature up or down in order to keep it within a comfortable range.

content.cn_text: 对食物的反馈也很好。
content.en_text: The feedback on the food was also positive.

content.cn_text: 以前空间站的宇航员曾形容饭菜很无聊。
content.en_text: Astronauts on previous Space stations had described the meals as boring.

content.cn_text: 但国际空间站的饭菜因其多样性而受到称赞。
content.en_text: But the International Space Station meals were praised for their variety.

content.cn_text: 所以，这是另一个很好的反馈。
content.en_text: So, that was another good piece of feedback.

content.cn_text: 今天被认为是理所当然的事情，但在那时却是士气的重要推动力，
content.en_text: And something, which is taken for granted today, but was a key morale booster in those days,

content.cn_text: 那就是空间站与地球之间的通讯，
content.en_text: was communication between the Space Station and Earth,

content.cn_text: 宇航员说这清晰可靠。
content.en_text: which the astronaut said was clear and reliable.

content.cn_text: 所以，在早期，气氛总体上似乎非常积极。
content.en_text: So, in the early days, it seems that the mood was overall very positive.

content.cn_text: 然而，几天后，困难和挫折变得更加明显。
content.en_text: However, after a few days, the difficulties and frustrations became clearer.

content.cn_text: 最重要的是，宇航员抱怨空气过滤器发出的持续噪音，
content.en_text: Above all, the crew complained about the constant noise caused by the air filters,

content.cn_text: 它们24小时不停地嘎嘎作响、嗡嗡作响，打断了他们的睡眠。
content.en_text: which used to rattle and buzz 24 hours a day, interrupting their sleep.

content.cn_text: 除此之外，宇航员们还抱怨魔术贴固定器，
content.en_text: As well as that, the astronauts complained that the Velcro fasteners,

content.cn_text: 这些遍布整个空间站的固定器效果不佳。
content.en_text: which were positioned all over the station, didn't work well.

content.cn_text: 因此，很多时候，舱内的物品都固定不住，而是四处漂浮。
content.en_text: And so, a lot of the time, objects weren't secure in the cabin, but floated about.

content.cn_text: 一个特别严重的问题是，空间站上使用的系统
content.en_text: A particularly serious problem was that the systems used on the Space Station

content.cn_text: 是由不同国家开发的，
content.en_text: had been developed in a number of different countries,

content.cn_text: 它们并不总是能很好地协同工作。
content.en_text: and they didn't always work well together.

content.cn_text: 例如，不同系统产生的水无法混合，
content.en_text: For example, it was impossible to mix the water produced from different systems,

content.cn_text: 因为它含有不同种类的防腐剂，
content.en_text: because it contained different sorts of preservatives,

content.cn_text: 这些防腐剂会相互反应并堵塞管道。
content.en_text: and these reacted together and blocked the pipes.

content.cn_text: 另一个问题与维修有关。
content.en_text: Another problem was connected to repairs.

content.cn_text: 在最好的情况下，这些维修也占用了空间站上过多的时间，
content.en_text: At the best of times, these took up an inordinate amount of time on the station,

content.cn_text: 宇航员抱怨他们得到的许多工具
content.en_text: and the crew complained that many of the tools that they'd been provided with

content.cn_text: 不适合他们必须完成的工作。
content.en_text: weren't suitable for the jobs they had to do.

content.cn_text: 所以，他们不得不即兴发挥，这意味着工作花费的时间比所需的长得多。
content.en_text: So, they had to improvise, which meant that jobs took a lot longer than they needed to.

content.cn_text: 另一个相关问题是难以接触到需要维护的设备。
content.en_text: Another related problem was to do with access to equipment that needed maintaining.

content.cn_text: 因为设备上的说明书经常放在一些地方，
content.en_text: Because the instructions on the equipment were often located in places,

content.cn_text: 在那里很难阅读，比如在设备的背面，
content.en_text: where they were difficult to read, like on the back of equipment,

content.cn_text: 通常很难或不可能阅读它们。
content.en_text: it was often difficult or impossible to read them.

content.cn_text: 因此，在确定了这些问题后，研究人员提出了一些建议，
content.en_text: So, having identified these problems, the researchers came up with a number of suggestions

content.cn_text: 关于如何在后续任务中改善条件。
content.en_text: about how conditions could be improved on subsequent missions.

content.cn_text: 首先，很明显，尽管与宇航员进行了大量的咨询，
content.en_text: First, it became clear that although there had been a great deal of consultation with the crews,

content.cn_text: 空间站的系统和设备需要进行更广泛的测试。
content.en_text: the systems and equipment on the space station needed much more wide-ranging testing.

content.cn_text: 然后，语言训练的整个问题也受到了审查。
content.en_text: Then, the whole issue of language training came under review.

content.cn_text: 来自不同国家的宇航员必须能够与地球上的控制员对话并理解他们。
content.en_text: Astronauts from different countries had to be able to talk to the controllers on Earth and to understand them.

content.cn_text: 尽管他们接受了广泛的语言训练，但宇航员有时仍有问题
content.en_text: Now, although they'd had extensive language training, the astronauts sometimes had problems

content.cn_text: 理解所说内容，因为他们不熟悉说话者的口音。
content.en_text: comprehending what was said because they weren't familiar with the accent of the speaker.

content.cn_text: 这有时会导致相当危险的误解。
content.en_text: And this sometimes led to quite dangerous misunderstandings.

content.cn_text: 最后，这也是我发现最令人惊讶的一点，
content.en_text: Finally, and this is the point I found most surprising,

content.cn_text: 研究人员认为空间站的设计者过于关注安全，
content.en_text: the researchers felt that the designers of the space station were paying too much attention to safety,

content.cn_text: 而不是考虑操作问题。
content.en_text: rather than considering operating issues.

content.cn_text: 也就是说，空间站如何才能舒适顺利地运行。
content.en_text: That is, how the station could be run comfortably and smoothly.

content.cn_text: 建议在此进行相当根本性的改变。
content.en_text: And the recommendation was to make changes here at quite a fundamental level.

content.cn_text: 所以，这些就是我涵盖的主要内容……
content.en_text: So, those were the main points I covered in the...

content.cn_text: 第四部分到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of part four.

content.cn_text: 你现在有一分钟时间来检查第四部分的答案。
content.en_text: You now have one minute to check your answers to part four.

content.cn_text: 谢谢。
content.en_text: Thank you.

content.cn_text: 听力测试到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of the listening test.

content.cn_text: 在雅思考试中，你现在将有十分钟时间将答案转写到答题卡上。
content.en_text: In the IELTS test, you would now have ten minutes to transfer your answers to the answer sheet. 69<

content.cn_text: 谢谢。
content.en_text: Thank you.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31–40

items.title: Difficulties with air filters caused lack of 34. ______
e.g. there were problems when mixing 35. ______ because of the preservatives
The 36. ______ available were not always suitable for their purpose
It was difficult to access the 37. ______ for equipment maintenance
Systems and equipment should undergo more extensive 38. ______
In language training, astronauts needed practice in understanding the controller’s 39. ______
There should be less emphasis on 40. ______ and more on other issues

groups.source_collect:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
Life on the International Space Station
Background
• Station was occupied by international teams of scientists over several months.
• Research was done on living conditions.
Early impressions
• The air smelt $31$
• The temperature was easy to adjust.
• The food had enough $32$
• $33$ was efficient.
Problems
• Difficulties with air filters caused lack of $34$
• It was difficult to keep cabin objects secure.
• Systems were incompatible
• e.g. there were problems when mixing $35$ because of the preservatives
• The $36$ available were not always suitable for their purpose
• It was difficult to access the $37$ for equipment maintenance
Suggested improvements
• Systems and equipment should undergo more extensive $38$
• In language training, astronauts needed practice in understanding the controller’s $39$
• There should be less emphasis on $40$ and more on other issues
